intro: Thomasia quercifolia, also known as the oak-leaf Thomasia, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Thomasia quercifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thomasia quercifolia is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to take advantage of its skin-soothing and wound-healing properties.
